one_liner: Gladly offers a broad, well-documented REST API (Basic auth via email + API token) for customer service data, but API access requires an existing paid enterprise account with admin-granted permissions rather than open self-serve signup.

auth_methods.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using Gladly login email as username and generated API token as password, sent via Authorization header over HTTPS.

self_serve_notes: No public free/self-serve developer tier for the core enterprise platform; API access requires an existing paid subscription/account with a user granted the 'API User' permission. Tokens are then self-generated via Settings > API Tokens. A separate SMB Shopify-app version offers a 30-day trial but is a different limited product."

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive REST API covering agents, conversations, customers, tasks, topics, teams, inboxes, exports, webhooks, reports, and a separate Lookup API. Also has webhook event system.

main_blocker: Requires an existing paid Gladly subscription and admin-granted 'API User' permission to generate API tokens; no public self-serve signup for API access.

evidence.supports: REST API surface, endpoints, and Basic Auth via Authorization header
How to generate API tokens and required permissions
Details on auth (Basic auth with email/token), endpoint list, and sandbox usage

flags: No public self-serve free tier; enterprise/contract-gated access
Third-party MCP server (StackOne) exists, not an official first-party Gladly MCP
Sandbox environment available at {organization}.gladly.qa for testing once account provisioned
